brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +53.6% since Feb 27 — Brent $109.03 (Apr 2 close); markets closed Apr 3-4; WTI-Brent spread inverted; Pakistan ceasefire talks collapsed Apr 3; F-15E shot down; Apr 6 deadline 2 days
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 2 close: $109.03/bbl (+7.8% session). Markets closed Apr 3-4 (Good Friday/weekend). WTI-Brent spread inverted — unusual structure reflecting acute US domestic supply anxiety tied to Hormuz closure. March +~55% — largest monthly gain since inception in 1988. Pakistan-mediated ceasefire talks collapsed Apr 3. F-15E Strike Eagle shot down over Iran Apr 3 — first US combat aircraft loss to enemy fire since 2003. Nobel laureate Krugman warns $150/bbl plausible. Apr 6 energy strike deadline 2 days away. Source: ICE Futures Europe / Bloomberg / Goldman Sachs.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +67.7% since Feb 27 — WTI $111.54 (Apr 2 close); first close above $100 since Jul 2022; WTI-Brent spread inverted; markets closed Apr 3-4; Bloomberg: Houthi Red Sea disruption could push oil to $140
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 2 close: $111.54/bbl (+$11.42, +11.4%). First close above $100 since July 2022. WTI-Brent spread inverted — reflecting acute US domestic supply anxiety. Gas at $4.091/gal nationally (Apr 4 AAA). Diesel $5.43/gal. California $5.92/gal. Markets closed Apr 3-4 (Good Friday/weekend). Bloomberg Economics: Houthi Red Sea disruption could push oil to $140/bbl since Saudi rerouted 5M bpd through Red Sea ports. IEA: 'largest supply disruption in history of global oil market' — 12M bpd lost. Apr 6 energy strike deadline 2 days away. Source: NYMEX / AAA / Bloomberg / Goldman Sachs.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$1.11 / +37.2% since Feb 27 — $4.091 nationally (Apr 4 AAA) — +$1.11 in 36 days — CA $5.92 — OK cheapest $3.29 — diesel CA $7.64 — largest monthly jump on record
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). Apr 4: $4.091/gal nationally (AAA) — $1.11 surge in 36 days. California leads at $5.92/gal; Hawaii $5.58; Washington $5.38; Oregon $4.98. Oklahoma cheapest at $3.29/gal; Kansas $3.36; North Dakota $3.46. Diesel in California reached $7.64/gal. National average crossed $4 for first time in four years. Monthly jump exceeds steepest climbs after Hurricane Katrina (2005) and Russia-Ukraine invasion (2022). Goldman: every $10/bbl crude increase adds ~$0.25/gal at pump. Source: AAA / EIA / Goldman Sachs.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -296 pts / -4.3% — S&P 6,582.69 (Apr 2 close); markets closed Apr 3-4; first weekly gain since conflict began (+3.4%); Dow 46,505; VIX 23.87; jobs report +178K (3x expectations)
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Apr 2 close: 6,582.69 (+0.11%). Markets closed Apr 3-4 (Good Friday). First weekly gain since conflict began (+3.4%). Dow 46,504.67. Nasdaq 21,879.18. VIX 23.87. March jobs report (released Good Friday): 178K vs 60K expected — nearly triple forecasts. Unemployment fell to 4.3%. CPI approaching 4%. Fed in impossible bind: strong jobs + surging inflation = zero rate cuts for 2026. Gold $4,702.70/oz. 10Y Treasury 4.31%. New tariffs Apr 2: 50% on steel/aluminum/copper, 100% on patented pharmaceuticals. National debt $39T. LMT $622.79 (+30% YTD). RTX ~$196 (+61% TTM). XOM ~$160.69. CVX ~$198.78. DAL ~$64. UAL ~$91.35. Source: NYSE / Bloomberg / CNBC / BLS.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$1.0-1.5B/day — F-15E shot down Apr 3 (first US combat aircraft loss since 2003) — A-10 also downed — 3 CSGs committed — ceasefire talks collapsed — Apr 6 deadline 2 days
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 36: ~$1.0-1.5B/day. F-15E Strike Eagle shot down over Iran Apr 3 — first US combat aircraft loss to enemy fire since 2003. Pilot rescued; WSO missing. A-10 Warthog also downed during rescue (pilot recovered). Two Black Hawks took small-arms fire. Pakistan-mediated ceasefire talks collapsed Apr 3 — Iran refused to meet US officials. Three CSGs committed to theater: Lincoln (Arabian Sea), Ford (Eastern Med), Bush (transiting Atlantic). Gen. Randy George forced into retirement by SecDef Hegseth. Apr 6 energy deadline 2 days. Pentagon $200B supplemental. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / CENTCOM / USNI / Reuters.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (36 Days)
total_cost.note: Pentagon $11.3B Day 6 + CSIS $16.5B Day 12 + ~$1-1.5B/day ongoing — ~$43.1B — Penn Wharton: $40-95B direct, up to $210B total economic impact — $200B supplemental: Massie 'Is this the first $200B?' — Murkowski won't fund without plan — Trump approval 35-39.7%
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12. Extrapolated through Day 36: ~$43.05B. Penn Wharton Budget Model: $40B-$95B in direct costs, up to $210B total economic impact. $200B supplemental — more than 20% of annual DOD budget. Rep. Massie: 'Is this the first $200 billion?' Sen. Murkowski won't fund more without plan. Trump approval: Silver Bulletin 39.7% / CNN 35% — first time below 40% in second term. War approval: 38% support vs 55% oppose (Silver Bulletin). 61% disapprove of Trump handling (Pew). Source: Pentagon / CSIS / Penn Wharton / CNN / Pew.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CENTCOM: 15,000+ targets — over 5,000 total targets struck — EA-37B Compass Call II first operational use — 24 F-22s at Israel's Ovda — GBU-72 5,000-lb penetrators — 850+ Tomahawks
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM/Pentagon: over 5,000 targets struck. Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~35,000+ through Day 36. EA-37B Compass Call II electronic warfare aircraft deployed Apr 2 — first-ever operational use. 24 F-22 Raptors at Israel's Ovda Airbase (first US offensive weapons in Israel). ~35 F-15E Strike Eagles in Jordan. 30 A-10s (18 more deploying). B-52Hs from RAF Fairford (20 JDAMs each). GBU-72 5,000-lb penetrator munitions against underground silos. 850+ Tomahawk cruise missiles fired. Source: CENTCOM / IDF / Pentagon / CSIS.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 13-15 US KIA — F-15E Strike Eagle shot down Apr 3 (first US combat aircraft loss since 2003) — pilot rescued, WSO missing — A-10 also downed (pilot recovered) — 2 Black Hawks took fire
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. 13-15 KIA depending on source: Trump cited 13 in April 1 speech; CENTCOM reports 15 including non-combat. F-15E Strike Eagle shot down over Iran Apr 3 — first confirmed US combat aircraft loss to enemy fire since 2003. One pilot rescued; weapons systems officer missing with search operations underway. A-10 Warthog also downed during rescue attempt (pilot recovered). Two Black Hawks took small-arms fire. Key prior incidents: 6 soldiers KIA Camp Arifjan Kuwait (Mar 1); 6 airmen KIA KC-135 crash Iraq (Mar 12). Wounded: 303 per CENTCOM, potentially 520+ per The Intercept. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / The Intercept / Stars and Stripes.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: 303-520+ WIA (CENTCOM vs. The Intercept) — 315 RTD ~91% — F-15E/A-10 losses Apr 3 raise air defense concern — Gen. George forced to retire — SecDef Hegseth fired 12+ senior leaders
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. CENTCOM: 303 WIA; The Intercept: potentially 520+. Pentagon Mar 31 briefing: 348 WIA (315 returned to duty ~91%; 6 remain seriously wounded). F-15E and A-10 losses on Apr 3 demonstrate Iran's improved air defense effectiveness. SecDef Hegseth forced Army Chief Gen. Randy George into immediate retirement Apr 2-3. Over a dozen senior military leaders fired since Hegseth took office. Pentagon rebranded as 'Department of War.' Navy: 292 ships (106 deployed). Total US forces ~50,000. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / USNI / Reuters.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (est.)
iranian_killed.note: Health Min: 1,800+ killed, 23,000+ wounded — HRANA: 3,461+ (1,551 civilians, 236 children) — Iran Int'l: 4,700+ — FM Araghchi: repeated Bushehr strikes risk radioactive fallout — 3.2M displaced
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — dramatic discrepancies across sources reflecting fog of war and 30+ day internet blackout. Iranian Health Ministry: 1,800+ killed, 23,000+ injured since Feb 28. HRANA independently documented 3,461+ deaths including 1,551 civilians. Iran International: 4,700+ security forces killed. Combined extrapolated estimate: ~8,200+. Half of Iran's missile launchers remain intact per US intelligence. Iran shifting from saturation attacks to fewer, heavier missiles with 1,000+ kg payloads. FM Araghchi warned Apr 4 that repeated Bushehr strikes risk radioactive fallout reaching GCC capitals. 83,351 residential buildings damaged. 3.2 million displaced. IAEA severed since Feb 26. Source: Iran Health Ministry / HRANA / Iran International / OCHA / IAEA.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 1,345+ killed — 3,935+ wounded — ~1M displaced (20% of pop.) — 200,000+ crossed into Syria — 132,000+ in 620+ shelters at 94% capacity — IDF: disarming Hezbollah 'unrealistic'
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli strikes and ground invasion since March 2. 1,345+ killed per Lebanese MoH. 3,935+ wounded. ~1 million displaced (20% of population). 200,000+ crossed into Syria. 132,000+ in 620+ collective shelters at 94% capacity. 49 primary healthcare centers and 5 hospitals shut following Israeli evacuation orders. IDF reframed war goals Apr 3 — acknowledged militarily disarming Hezbollah 'unrealistic' without occupying all of Lebanon. Lebanese govt proscribed Hezbollah military activities. Lebanon Flash Appeal: $308.3M requested. Source: Lebanon Health Ministry / IDF / UNHCR / WHO.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: WHO: 4.3M displaced across region — Iran 3.2M — Lebanon ~1M (20% of pop.) — 200,000+ Lebanese into Syria — WHO flash appeal $30.3M — 115M need humanitarian aid across Eastern Med
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. WHO estimates 4.3 million displaced across the region. Iran: ~3.2 million internally displaced. Lebanon: ~1 million (20% of population), with 200,000+ crossing into Syria. WHO issued $30.3M flash appeal (Mar-Aug 2026). 115 million people across Eastern Mediterranean require humanitarian assistance — nearly half of all people in need globally. WHO humanitarian health appeals 70% underfunded. WHO global logistics hub in Dubai disrupted by airspace restrictions — 50+ emergency supply requests delayed, benefiting 1.5M across 25 countries. 18 verified attacks on Iranian healthcare facilities. Source: WHO / UNHCR / OCHA.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — ~2,000 vessels stranded — ~20,000 seafarers — Hormuz ~6 transits/day — Iran-Oman transit protocol under negotiation — Philippine-flagged vessels allowed
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28. ~2,000 vessels and ~20,000 seafarers stranded. Hormuz traffic down to ~6 transits/day from 130+ pre-conflict. Iran-Oman transit protocol under negotiation (Apr 2 — triggered market reversal). Iran agreed to allow Philippine-flagged vessels through following bilateral talks. IRGC 'tollbooth' at Larak Island charging up to $2M per crossing in yuan or crypto. Mar 1-25: only 142 total transits — down 93%. Source: IMO / IATA / Lloyd's / Bloomberg / Windward.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: HRANA: 236+ children (Iran) + Lebanon 124+ + Israel 4 + Kuwait 1 — IRGC cluster bombs wounding children in Bnei Brak — Bushehr struck 4 times — Araghchi: radioactive fallout risk
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. HRANA: 236+ children killed in Iran. Lebanon: 124+ children (updated Lebanese MoH). Israel: 4. Kuwait: 1. IRGC cluster-warhead missiles continue targeting Israeli cities — children critically wounded in Bnei Brak. Minab school (Feb 28): 165-170 killed, mostly girls aged 7-12. 600+ educational centers attacked in Iran per Red Crescent. Bushehr NPP struck 4 times during Epic Fury — FM Araghchi warned Apr 4 repeated strikes risk radioactive fallout reaching GCC capitals. Plant holds 72 tons of fuel and 210 tons of spent fuel. Source: UNICEF / HRANA / Lebanese MoH / UNHRC / ICRC.
